columns.content:
Brands are built and fueled by communication.

Everything communicates so naturally, everyone at SEB plays a role in how our brand is perceived and valued in the minds of our customers.

SEB’s visual identity has a big impact on creating the desired perception of our brand. A simple and uncluttered visual system plays a crucial part in making our messages cut through the noise.

sections.section-content:
### To create a consistent visual identity that draws from our legacy and our position, we have a few organizing principles:

## Modern, yet timeless.

Elegant, uncluttered, and effortless.  
Confidence. Less is more.  
Attention to detail. Just like in our business.

sections.title: Making ourselves heard
sections.section-content: Some media environments are noisier than others and we need to make sure our message comes across as intended.

columns.title: High noise level
columns.content:
When we are situated in a very noisy media environment we need to take our rightful space. On a high level the logo is bigger, the texts are bigger and punchier, and CTA's are shorter and sweeter.

– This applies to external and paid media such as OOH, digital OOH, digital ads, social media, print, etc.

columns.title: Mid noise level
columns.content:
At mid-level, we already have the viewer's attention. We can tone down and talk in a regular speaking voice. Sometimes we speak a bit louder to get our message across, but we never scream. Still, short and to the point.

– This applies to our website start page, presentations, education, etc.

columns.title: Low noise level
columns.content:
At low-level, we have longer time than usual – a chance to provide in-depth information to our clients to make them truly understand our offering.

– This applies to our app, product pages, documents, letters, sub-pages on website, etc.

columns.caption: Low-level – Cover of SEB Annual report.
Set of icons used in communication.
Mid-level – Value Magazine 2021 – Gold in Swedish Design Awards.
